content.content: Content Strategy  ·  Experience Strategy  ·  AI-Enabled Content Operations
Information Architecture  ·  LLM-Aware SEO  ·  Engagement & Delivery

15+ years shaping digital experiences for Fortune 500 clients across pharmaceutical, healthcare, finance, automotive, and CPG. I design content and experience strategy for complex, regulated environments — from information architecture and taxonomy to CMS-ready manuscripts, editorial governance, and AI-enabled content operations.

I build LLM-aware SEO foundations, prompt libraries, and human-in-the-loop review systems that connect AI-assisted drafting with expert review at enterprise scale.
Audits, frameworks, editorial governance, voice & tone, messaging pillars for regulated environments.
Audience journeys, information architecture, taxonomy design, content modeling, CMS architecture.
CMS-ready manuscripts, page and module architecture, UX writing, content specifications.
Technical SEO, schema & structured data, LLM-aware SEO, and zero-click GEO / AEO patterns.
Prompt libraries, AI-assisted audits and audience analysis, agentic drafting + expert review workflows.
SOW, P&L, multi-discipline teams of 5–10, QBRs, risk management, and account growth.

Largest client P&L
owned and delivered
Successful website migrations and redesigns delivered since 2016

Account growth for UC Davis
Health, with one phase leading to six
Vertical expertise in Automotive, Financial Services, Healthcare, Pharmaceuticals, CPG, and SaaS
Reduction in timelines and level of effort with Generative AI technology
A repeatable approach, honed over 15 years.

AI-assisted content audits to find what's working and what's missing. Interviews with subject matter experts and internal teams, audience research, persona development, competitive benchmarking, and analytics review. By the end of this phase, there's a shared understanding of the current state and the gaps.

Information architecture, taxonomy, content models, visual sitemaps, editorial frameworks, voice and tone guidelines, and messaging pillars. This is the structural work that determines how content gets organized and written before anyone opens a CMS.

CMS-ready manuscripts, module architecture, UX writing, schema and structured data implementation, LLM-aware SEO, and GEO/AEO strategy. Everything built here ties back to the architecture from phase two. Nothing ships without a defined content model behind it.

Editorial workflows, MLR and compliance review, human-in-the-loop quality checks for AI-generated content, lifecycle management, and performance iteration. This phase also covers the sell-through for next phases, because organic account growth is paramount.
Building content systems ready for the LLM era.
I help clients show up in AI-generated answers, not just search results. That means rethinking how content is structured, marked up, and written.

Search is splitting into two channels: traditional rankings and AI-generated citations. I build for both. Schema markup tuned for entity recognition, structured data that maps to how models retrieve and cite sources, and content architectures that answer the intent patterns LLMs surface, not just the keywords Google ranks.

Most teams using AI for content are improvising every prompt from scratch. Inconsistent output, no way to compare results. I build standardized prompt sets for content audits, audience analysis, and rendering reviews that any strategist can run and get reliable output. The prompts encode the methodology so the work scales.

AI drafts fast but gets things wrong in ways that matter. Off-brand language, unsupported claims, regulatory violations. I design rubric-based scoring systems between AI output and publication. Each draft gets evaluated against accuracy, brand voice, and the compliance requirements of the client's industry.

The full pipeline from draft to published asset. AI handles what it's good at, humans own what it isn't. AI generates drafts, subject matter experts check accuracy, MLR approves for compliance, and the asset publishes through defined handoffs. Every step has clear entry and exit criteria.
Fifteen+ years delivering content strategies at scale.
New York, NY (Remote) — Fortune 500 content & experience strategy; AI-first content operations.
Franklin, TN (Remote) — Global taxonomy, IA, and AEM migration leadership.
Atlanta, GA — Nissan / INFINITI, large global AEM implementation.
San Francisco, CA — Consumer & business banking digital experience.
Los Angeles · Pleasanton · San Francisco · San Mateo · Sunnyvale

title: My processes can be tailored by project type to accommodate a range of solutions and budgets. The process illustrated over the next set of slides contains typical steps for a large-scale website, application, or omnichannel experience. I have also included behind-the-scenes snapshots of key steps within the process.

content.content:
Competitive Landscape
Channel / Platform
Analytics / Data Insights
Content, UX, & Design Evaluation

Search Behavior Analysis
Search Volume Analysis

Test & Learn
Surveys and PollsFeedback Mechanisms

Matching audience needs to features & functionality
Defining the cross-channel user journey

Vision & Experience Strategy 
Content & Editorial Strategy 
Feature Concepts 
Roadmap

Wireframes 
Prototypes 
Design Systems 
Development 
UAT

Outlines the priorities, requirements, and overall goals of the project. Establishes roles, responsibilities, and expectations. Includes document review, client interviews, technical audit and analysis.

Draws out relevant needs, opportunities, behaviors, and desires based on a detailed UX, design, and content inventory, audit, and analysis mixed with available site analytics, search behavior analysis, usability testing, and any other data sources.
Qualitative and quantitative audit and analysis of existing content. Provides a picture of the current communications platform including owned channels and channels of similar organizations.
Defines key audience personas, journeys, and user needs adding in data-driven attributes to provide the most accurate depiction of those that will consume and engage with the content and experience.

Establishes a clearly-defined vision, content + editorial strategy, experience strategy, SEO / SEM approach to content creation, as well as feature set requirements, drawing on data-informed insights from earlier in the process.
Establish the site architecture (sitemap and site shell) based on approved and aligned upon features and requirements.
Defines templates, components, design batches, the review process, and timeline for wireframes, designs, and development.

Sets the voice and tone, color, typography, illustration, imagery, photography, color palette, and user interface elements. This could be based on an existing brand style guide or created from scratch.

Defines the structure, functionality, content, and visual design at the template and component level, based on the approved features, structure, and design plan. This often includes a copy deck and metadata recommendations.

Creation of all required build kit assets (annotations, designs, copy deck, style guide, analytics tagging specifications, SEO documentation, etc.) development guidance, and support to build the experience against my specifications.

Sets the project up for success by establishing KPIs, analytics event tagging specifications, and measurement framework to ensure I can measure the right things to help make data-informed decisions and adjustments post-launch.
Builds a working website from the designs and previous site mapping work. (or I can partner with client-side IT or development teams to build this site within an existing infrastructure).
Provides quality assurance and user acceptance testing of all key systems, designs, and content ahead of the launch to ensure a stable and fully-functional experience.
Ideally, pending scope, I iterate, measure, optimize, and expand from this point forward dependent on user data and client needs.

OUTPUT: 
Internal Brief, Workback Plan (Smartsheet), and Solution Plan
Understand business requirements, establish goals, and get immersed in the brand. Align on roles and responsibilities, brief, and solution + project plan.

Synthesized discovery brief
Business objectives
Project north star and inspiration
Areas of focus and exploration
Technical mandatories
Plan of action
Speak with key internal client leads and/or have a client workshop to understand:

Goals and objectives for the design 
Audience identification and prioritization 
Initial onboarding questions 
Mandatories and requirements 
Current and future initiatives that could affect the website 
Pain points and needs related to the current websites 
What success looks like
Analyze traffic, engagement, pathways, and conversion across digital properties to understand:

Who is visiting the properties? 
When are they visiting and on what devices? 
What are the top performing pages and content? 
What content and features are most highly engaged?
What are their journeys within and between digital properties? 
What are the regional nuances in site traffic and engagement?
Analyze findability, tagging, linking, site architecture, and URL structure of my digital properties to understand:

How are visitors finding these properties? 
Where are they coming from? 
What are the search behaviors for target audiences? 
What are top questions being asked in the space? 
What are keywords or phrases I can own? 
How does it differ by region?
Audit and index features, functionality, and content on existing sites and similar experiences to understand:

What features functionality, and content exist in the platform?
What tools, services, and data feeds exist in the platform 
Functionality and content for specific features on comparable or competitive experiences 
Opportunities and gaps in how features and content match-up with community audience needs
Collect and analyze data directly from users via surveys, usability testing, and/or interviews to understand:

What are my visitors trying to achieve? 
What hurdles are they experiencing? 
What are their expectations for content and features?
What demographic information will they share? 
How might I begin to categorize my visitors and review their responses in a structured way?
Using audience research and audit findings, develop profiles and journeys to illustrate:

Key mindsets, needs, goals, and current pain-points
Content, features, experiences, and resources that best meet audience needs
Opportunities to engage with target audiences over time across touchpoints
The experience strategy is the culmination of insights uncovered during discovery and research into a north star vision of what the new experience should solve for. This often includes:

Experience imperatives or principles
Short-term vision 
Long-term vision
Feature definition & requirements
I develop a well-defined strategy for content, editorial, and SEO across channels, using search data to build a long-term plan for creating, delivering, and managing high-value content.

Content Themes & Pillars
Content Needs & Opportunities
Content Formats & Style
Content Distribution Channels
Governance and Workflow Guidelines

The SEO recommendations are the culmination of findings from the technical audit, alignment with strategy, and insights from immersion sessions. I create a complete framework for clients that improves visibility and conversion.

Striking Distance Keywords
New Keyword Opportunities
Backlink Recommendations 
Site Performance Recommendations

The sitemap is a representation of the full platform architecture. It accounts for all pages, content, tools, and actions that need to live within the system. It shows how sections and pages are organized, their hierarchy, and where key content lives within the structure.
The digital design system defines the look and feel of all elements to be included in the new digital experience. It often includes:

Voice and tone 
Color and typography 
Imagery and illustration 
User interface elements 
Motion and animation
Site shell (header, footer, nav)

Grounded in the features and requirements defined in the previous phases, execution moves swiftly. I create digital experiences that are high performing, beautiful, and functional, while faithfully representing the brand.

Enterprise websites and microsites
CRM design
Application design
Tools & Intranets

Creating end-to-end content, from ideation and keyword research to writing on-brand content. My process simplifies client reviews and approvals, ensuring every piece of copy is strategically aligned and resonates with your target audience.

Voice & Tone Guidelines
Copy Deck Templates
Completed Copy Decks
Build Kits

OUTPUT: 
Workflows, Process Documents, and Governance Frameworks

I create Governance Plans that establish the framework for managing content creation, approval, publication, and maintenance to ensure consistency, quality, and compliance. I also create Content Lifecycle Plans that outline the stages through which content passes from creation to archiving. This plan ensures that content remains relevant, accurate, and valuable over time.

I know a brand doesn’t end at their owned experience. I work tirelessly to make sure that a brand’s tone of voice, visual branding, and look and feel are consistent across all touchpoints and media. I create:

Paid media assets
Social media assets
Graphic elements and creative exploration
Print media needs and resources

title: My process is built around audience data, tight timelines, and budget discipline. Projects ship on time.

content.content: Nine selected engagements across pharmaceutical, healthcare, finance, automotive, and CPG.
Re-imagining the owned digital experience for an academic health system
Content strategy, taxonomy, and IA across a multi-client academic health system.

THE SITUATION
UC Davis Health is a leading, nationally ranked academic health system that tackles the world’s most pressing healthcare issues. They provide patient care, research trailblazing therapies, and offer world-class medical education. Their owned digital platform is a collection of academic, clinical, and research department microsites, each with their own client leads and goals. The site architecture and navigation was largely based on internal organizational structure, department initiatives, and leadership requests, creating siloed experiences that made it difficult for their priority audiences, including prospective patients, referring physicians, and prospective residents, to navigate to relevant content easily. They needed a phased and systematic process to overhauling their site, that could get buy-in from across the organization.
Content strategy, taxonomy, and IA across a multi-phase redesign. Grew the account from one phase to six.

notes:
They are a nationally ranked health system, providing patient care, research, and world-class medical education…Their owned digital platform is a collection of clinical, academic, and research microsites. And their biggest initial problem was that the site structure was based on internal organization structure and internal team requests, which did not match the mindsets of their target audiences, making it difficult for them to get to relevant content, resources and actions.
Goals were to acquire patients, elevate national reputation, illustrate research and treatments, and recruit residents, faculty & fellows

content.content:
MY APPROACH
Partnering with their internal marketing team, I took an audience-centered and data-driven approach to re-imagining the UC Davis site network over several phases, ultimately providing the team with a revised design system, new templates, tools, and governance guidelines that they have used to develop and roll out across the site, giving all site editors the ability to create on-brand and engaging content. The process started with multiple rounds of interviews with department heads, followed by deep content, experience, and performance audits, multiple rounds of diagnostic user testing, as well as prototype and tree-testing new designs and navigational models. These critical data inputs were used to inform a system-wide new vision, features, and revised approach to their global taxonomy.

notes:
RYAN

I took a very data and user-driven approach to tackle this ask, performing interviews with department heads, multiple rounds of user testing, and tree testing. 


reimagined and augmented their digital design system, providing new templates, tools, and governance guidelines that their team and editors are continuing to roll out sitewide.

content.content: I have transformed the digital experience…

An organization-centric experience with long form content, containing limited pathways for priority audiences (patients, prospective residents, fellows, faculty, and referring physicians) to quickly get to relevant information, services, and programs. Users were often taken directly to department sites, without clear connections or guidance, causing friction and disorientation.

A cohesive and smooth audience-focused experience with intuitive navigation and scannable content that guides users to health information, services, academic programs and resources, while answering questions and providing clear paths to getting support.

notes: RYANWe have transformed them FROM an organization-centric experience with long form content and limited pathways TO a cohesive and smooth audience-centric experience with intuitive navigation and scannable content that guides users to health information, services, academic programs and resources, while providing clear pathways to support.

content.content:
SITEWIDE NAVIGATION
Solves for the relationships between sub-brands, departments and units, allowing the move through the health system in intuitive ways.
SITEWIDE TAXONOMY
I defined new content types and global taxonomy, enabling dynamic content.
HEALTH LIBRARY
Allows easier pathways towards care and resources, while increasing search equity and communicating key differentiators
CLINIC FINDER TOOL
New finder and detail pages migrate previous static location information into a dynamic experience that provides the ability to quickly find relevant clinics and services.
NEW CONTENT MODULES
I created new content modules and components, for a more scannable reading experience. DYNAMIC LISTING PAGES
Searchable with lettered side navigation allow audiences to quicklytarget relevant programs, conditions, treatments, and services.
ACTION MODULES
These modules elevate and organize the most important actions for audiences.
ACADEMIC PROGRAM PAGES
Provide an overview of the residency, rotations, current residents, reasons to believe, and easy access to apply for a specific academic program.

SOLUTION & RESULTS
I delivered a new global navigation model, site templates, governance guidelines, and workflows to be leveraged across the organization. Key features and improvements include:
I delivered scalable features and tools to equip internal teams

notes: RYANWe also delivered scalable features and tools to equip their internal teams. Here we’re listing some of the key features that we launched and on the next slide, initial results from the launch of the new patients & visitors site, as well as reactions from user testing.

RECENT LAUNCH DATA
Initial data from the recent launch of the new Patients & Visitors site.

USER SATISFACTION
On average, the users tested were very satisfied with the new experience.
I delivered scalable features and tools to equip internal teams

content.content: A multi-year content audit, sitemap redesign, and IA overhaul for a high-compliance public health environment.
WEBSITE REDESIGN  ·  CONTENT WORKFLOWS  ·  CONTENT & ASSET CREATION

THE SITUATION
The United States Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Department of Viral Hepatitis (DVH) engaged my team to evolve and redesign their website, better ensuring a user-centered experience consistent with their strategic objectives. Midway through my engagement with DVH, CDC announced “Clean Slate”, an agency-wide directive to rethink, rewrite, and relaunch a new, improved, and redesigned CDC.gov website — all within the span of a year. Now, I needed to help DVH execute the Clean Slate directive to rewrite and re-platform their entire site while still delivering on the initial goals of their site evolution.

MY APPROACH
I executed thorough user testing on the DVH site to understand how both health care providers and public audiences reacted to website content, organization, and flow. Taking insights from user testing, I then audited the entire site from a content strategy, UX, SEO, and analytics lens to recommend a brand-new sitemap that adhered to Clean Slate’s limitations, templates, and site model expectations. I shepherded DVH through a massive project to rewrite 117 pages over a 6-month period, all of which needed to be cleared through communications and scientific leads at the CDC.

THE RESULTS
User testing outputs that delivered powerful insights guiding my future content and sitemap evolution 
1,300+ page site audit concluded with a 75% reduction in URL count, gaining DVH operational efficiencies in site maintenance with no impact to site traffic or user experience 
117 pages rewritten over a 6-month period to hit the Clean Slate relaunch date, all of which needed to be cleared through communications and scientific leads at CDC
I have transformed the digital experience…

A center and internally focused website with highly complex, dense content that isn’t tailored to their respective audiences. The site was ridden with duplicate content and assets, a result of the ”publish and forget” tone of the organization. Content contained conflicting information, outdated statistics, and lacked structure. Pages were not scannable and lacked a sense of uniformity.

A uniform, audience-centric experience with a global taxonomy that culminates in an intuitive topic-specific navigation framework and smoothly connects audiences to relevant health topics. Breaking down organizational silos in lieu for a network of websites centered around plain language queries.

CONTENT MODELS
Developed division-specific content models for their core content pages, creating a consistent and streamlined experience across audiences.
GLOBAL TAXONOMY
I defined a new global taxonomy for the division that connects audiences with relevant information, reducing the chances of user abandonment.

CONTENT REDUCTION
Significantly reduced content volume and duplicative pages. Consolidating similar pages and purging outdated information to create funneled user journeys.
STRUCTURED DATA
The new experience prioritizes modular components, paired with uniform headings and hierarchy. Helping audiences easily scan pages across the CDC site.
DYNAMIC RESOURCES
Transition from static resources to a dynamic, filterable, and searchable experience. While grouping audience and language versions for easier browsing.

PLAIN LANGUAGE
Transition from highly clinical and complex content to easily readable prose that is tailored to audiences. Creating a scannable, easy to digest experience.

USER CENTRIC
I delivered over 5 new site models leveraging uniform content templates across over 3 disease areas across patient, healthcare provider, and public health audiences.
New content models and templates to address patient, provider, and public health audiences

notes: I delivered over 5 new site models using uniform content templates across over 3 disease areas across patient, healthcare provider, and public health audiences.

content.content:
CONTENT INVENTORY
I compiled all content sources to create a master inventory inclusive of all pages, PDFs, and assets. Using site analytics and SEO data to make informed recommendations.

CLIENT REVIEW
Working with CDC division leaders and communicators, facilitate the review of manuscripts. Inclusive of managing conflicting and sometimes technical feedback.
CONTENT AUDIT
Liaise with client leads to identify pages and assets to keep, improve, or remove. Conducting workshops to help the division make data and insight-driven decisions.

SME REVIEW
Once content was entered in the CMS and undergone QA, facilitate the review of pages with division subject matter experts. Addressing feedback until the page was approved.

CONTENT PLANNING
Establish clear messaging hierarchy via template-specific page models. Culminating in content outlines and manuscript shells for writers to populate and revise.

CLIENT FACING
I worked with client leads to facilitate the planning, creation, and review of content across the project lifecycle leveraging complete, custom workflows.
I developed reliable workflows to facilitate content review and publishing at scale
A global website redesign that aligns to 30 years of innovation
Reimagined the corporate digital presence as the tangible illustration of three decades of innovation.

THE SITUATION
For more than 30 years, Acadia has been trailblazing breakthroughs for people with neurological conditions. They developed and commercialized the first and only approved treatment for hallucinations and delusions associated with Parkinson’s disease psychosis, and Rett syndrome. 
Acadia’s existing website was outdated, heavily clinical, and static, failing to connect with patients and provide a complete view of the company. To reflect the ‘new’ Acadia, they needed a new corporate website that embodies their new brand, providing the flexibility to grow with the organization for years to come.

THE SOLUTION
The new Acadia.com is the tangible illustration of the research and progress Acadia is making, with stories, insights, and information demonstrating why the new solutions matter to patients. The site balances clinical and patient-focused content with rich interactive features and stories within a user-centric architecture that is easy to navigate.
Transforming the Acadia corporate digital presence
A website that just covers the basics, feels outdated and static, doesn’t provide visitors the whole picture of who Acadia is, and fails to connect with patients.
A website that is the tangible illustration of the research and progress Acadia is making, with stories, insights, and information demonstrating why the new solutions matter to patients.

POSITIVE RESULTS
Overall increase in pageviews, users, organic clicks, and impressions. Referral Sources sessions increased by 3.6%.
I delivered features that fulfilled previously unmet audience needs

INTERACTIVE PRODUCT PIPELINE
The new filterable pipeline quickly visualizes the progress Acadia is making. Users can click cards that link to more information and resources.
INSIGHTS & STORIES HUB
This section brings together thought leadership, R&D, patient stories, and innovation into a rich editorial experience that brings Acadia to life.
THERAPEUTIC AREA PAGES
These pages were evolved to be more complete views of the disease, connecting to pipeline items and resources for patients.
LEADERSHIP PROFILES
Content-rich profiles give a full picture of Acadia's people, their expertise and thought leadership.
Connect and inspire through dynamic digital storytelling and meaningful engagement
Designed the content architecture and migration strategy consolidating 34 global sites. Secured $500K additional funding.
GLOBAL WEBSITE REDESIGN  ·  PRODUCT ROADMAP  ·  CREATIVE ASSETS

BACKGROUND
Mars is a private and family-owned business that owns some of the world's most recognizable brands across 3 core business units in Petcare, Snacking, and Nutrition. Touching every aspect of the product supply chain, Mars operates in 450+ locations in 80 different countries, and supports native local languages on their market destinations. 
Over the past several years, I have partnered with Global communications, Brand, and Marketing teams at Mars as well as integrated partners, to support severallarge initiatives, including a full-scale site redesign, global site architecture, ongoing digital roadmap, brand storytelling, DE&I, Sustainability, and paid media planning, strategy and content.

ENGAGEMENT
Across all my work I have helped Mars build a positive reputation by revolutionizing the Mars digital experience through dynamic storytelling that engages my target audiences in meaningful ways across my digital properties. Throughout my multi-year partnership, I’ve developed thousands of creative assets for the brand. Key engagements include: 
Led the digital strategy, and supporting discovery and user research to inform a three year+ roadmap 
Created a “One Mars.com” product team with strong processes, tools, and team collaboration 
Established the market website architecture restructure for best-in-class global to local approach 
Created a digital experience leveraging Site Studio (CMS), making it faster and easier to manage and govern content for Mars.com and market sites 
Evolved Mars.com experience and creative with 15+ new pages, new site navigation, over 500 creative assets and new immersive experiences (e.g., interactive sustainability storytelling) and launching a complete set of 45 new components and features

A limited, static, and outdated experience with a single-minded focus on brand equity initiatives and corporate communications priorities, containing long-form content, few visuals, dead-ends and little brand information.

A strong, interactive, and multi-faceted digital experience that tells the story of the whole company—its people, products, services, and purpose, engaging target users in ways that drive them to the right content and resources.

DIGITAL REVOLUTION
Revolutionizing the Mars.com digital experience through dynamic storytelling and new features that engage the target audiences in meaningful ways.
I have consistently delivered positive results
Destigmatizing ED and delivering hope to men living with ED via the first OTC treatment in the US
Launched the first FDA-cleared OTC gel for ED in the U.S. market with an empathetic, user-focused digital experience.
U.S. WEBSITE REDESIGN  ·  CRM PROGRAM  ·  CREATIVE ASSETS

THE SITUATION
Eroxon, the first FDA-cleared over-the-counter (OTC) gel for erectile dysfunction (ED), aimed to establish a strong presence in the U.S. market with its launch scheduled for Q4 2024. My challenge was to develop a pre-launch landing page and a complete website that effectively communicates the product’s unique benefits, educates consumers, and resonates with men experiencing ED as well as their partners. This digital initiative needed to differentiate Eroxon from traditional ED treatments while building trust and engagement within a competitive landscape dominated by oral medications.
THE OPPORTUNITY
The launch of Eroxon in the U.S. market presented a unique opportunity to introduce a revolutionary, non-invasive ED treatment that offers rapid results without the side effects associated with oral medications. Unlike its competitors, Eroxon works within 10 minutes, making it ideal for spontaneous use, and is designed to be discreet and easy to apply. The challenge was to clearly communicate these benefits while addressing common concerns around efficacy and safety, and to engage both users and their partners in a meaningful way.

THE APPROACH
My approach to the Eroxon launch centered on creating an educational, empathetic, and user-focused digital experience that highlighted the product’s unique features while addressing the emotional and relational aspects of ED. The pre-launch landing page built anticipation and drove pre-orders with streamlined navigation, clear calls-to-action, and benefit-focused messaging. For the full site launch, I added a thorough content library on ED causes, symptoms, and treatments to give users the knowledge they need in a positive, reassuring tone. I also engaged partners of ED patients through articles on the impact of ED on relationships and ways to offer support. Additionally, an FAQ section addressed common safety and efficacy concerns, ensuring users had the information needed to make informed decisions.
Immediately upon launch, I achieved a strong reach with incredible results

INTERACTIVE COMPARISON TOOL
Equips users to compare Eroxon with other ED treatments side-by-side, highlighting key advantages. This tool helps users make informed decisions with a clear understanding of how Eroxon stands out.

PARTNER-INCLUSIVE CONTENT AND JOURNEYS
The site features dedicated resources and articles for partners of ED patients, addressing the emotional and relational aspects of ED. This content is designed to foster communication and intimacy between partners.

INTERACTIVE ENGAGEMENT TOUCHPOINTS AND TOOLS
The website incorporates interactive elements, such as a product comparison tool and FAQs, making it easy for users to explore and understand the benefits of Eroxon compared to other ED treatments.

STUNNING, BOUNDARY-PUSHING DESIGNS
Delivers a visually engaging and smooth user experience that pushes the limits of the Haleon Web Framework. The design is modern and interactive, ensuring users enjoy a consistent and aesthetically pleasing journey across all devices.

FULL USER EDUCATION
The site provides extensive educational content on ED, including its causes, symptoms, and treatment options, helping users make informed decisions. The content is designed to be empathetic and informative, catering to both men and their partners.
Redesigning the MyQuit smoking cessation program for a new generation
Integrated MyQuit into Nicorette.com for one smooth cessation experience.
OMNICHANNEL STRATEGY  ·  DUAL BRAND INTEGRATION  ·  CONTENT AUDIT  ·  PRODUCT DESIGN

THE SITUATION
Nicorette has been stepping up its game with new gums, chews and lozenges, but their MyQuit program was stuck in the past – literally. Originally designed to help smokers quit, it had evolved haphazardly and piecemeal, becoming a tangled web of outdated applications, clunky websites, and confusing logged in experiences. 
Users found themselves lost in a maze of mismatched designs, redundant content, and overlapping platforms— trying to piece together an experience that simply didn't work anymore. For modern smokers ready to take the leap, the program felt more like a blocker than a helping hand. It was clear: MyQuit needed a serious refresh to meet today’s standards and truly support quitters on their journey.

THE APPROACH
After an extensive evaluation of the current Nicorette and MyQuit platforms, I outlined redundancies within the program—from a platform, content, and experience perspective.
Through that evaluation, I documented all content and experiences within the broken platform and outlined what was working, what wasn’t – and what should be migrated over into the new experience. 
I mapped out content and experiences to users at various stages of their quit smoking journey using both existing content as well as new gamification, tracking, and behavioral support tools. This full omnichannel experience, paired with a new visual identity, delivers on the current needs of users looking to quit smoking.
New features, platforms, content, and experiences to help users throughout their quit journey

INTEGRATION INTO NICORETTE.COM
As FDA standards were adjusted, I quickly determined that the Quit.com microsite, content, and logged-in experience needed to be incorporated into the Nicorette.com site. This absorption allowed one complete, streamlined experience for both cessation support AND product purchase.

A NEW MYQUIT VISUAL EXPERIENCE
With the new launch, I developed a fresh, new brand that not only brought the program into the 21st century, but also paired well with the new Nicorette rebrand that had recently been implemented.

JOURNEY STATES FOR ALL STATES OF SMOKING CESSATION
A smoker’s journey to quit is never linear –I made sure to identify, evaluate, and incorporate content, features, and platforms for all stages of a user’s journey from ready to quit, not quite ready, and even relapse journeys.
TOOLS THAT ACTUALLY WORK
I incorporated new tools into the MyQuit program that didn’t just offer lip service – they actually worked at actively helping a user to quit.

Content Alignment
I meticulously audited, categorized, and re-organized materials from both Nicorette as well as MyQuit. This created an intuitive and smooth content experience, where every piece of content was exactly where it needed to be – delivering clarity and value without confusion.

STREAMLINED PLATFORMS
By identifying redundancies and gaps in platforms and experiences that caused user confusion, I strategically combined platforms – eliminating overlaps, streamlining the experience, and creating a consistent intuitive journey for users.
Omnichannel strategy launching the first OTC topical gel for pain relief
Record-breaking results in usability, eCommerce conversion, and earned media.
OMNICHANNEL STRATEGY  ·  NEW PRODUCT WEBSITE  ·  CRM

THE SITUATION
With Voltaren transitioning from a prescription-only (RX) product to over-the-counter (OTC) availability, the brand faced a significant challenge: breaking into the highly competitive pain relief market dominated by well-established brands. To achieve high household penetration and increase awareness, Voltaren needed a complete and differentiated digital strategy that deeply resonated with arthritis consumers—a key target audience.
THE OPPORTUNITY
This transition presented a unique opportunity to create a first-of-its-kind digital experience tailored specifically for arthritis patients. By understanding the unique needs of this audience, the brand could build a website that not only provided information but also offered an inclusive, user-friendly environment that addressed the physical challenges faced by arthritis sufferers. This was a chance to set Voltaren apart from the competition by delivering real value to its users through new, accessible features.

THE APPROACH
I created a strong omnichannel strategy, inclusive of a pre-launch and full-launch version of the website and CRM program, with first-party data to encourage pre-orders in a first-of-its-kind eCommerce integration. Once in market, I introduced VoltarenGel.com—the first arthritis-friendly digital experience, recognized by The Arthritis Foundation and rigorously tested with real arthritis patients. 
The full site offered brand-new eCommerce functionality and first-party data collection. The site was built with a focus on accessibility, integrating features that were directly informed by the needs of its users. Voice-activated search and tap-to-chat functionalities, large tap targets, strategic page scroll depth, and hands-free content navigation via videos and head gesture scroll education were all critical components. These features made the site not just a digital destination, but a complete support system for arthritis patients.
Record-breaking results in usability, eCRM conversions, and eCommerce success

Voice-activated site search and voice enabled tap-to-chat capabilities, being used by 1/3 of all users in the first month post-launch. 

Strategic page scroll depth, large tap targets, and text resizing. 

Testing with arthritis patients which showed that 99% of users found the website easy to use. 
Pre-order eCommerce week 1 was noted by Shoppable as the highest one-week total sales of any brand.

Ability to view hands-free content via videos and head gesture scroll education.

Build credibility and awareness around optimized experience, endorsed by the Arthritis Foundation. 


Over 61,500 users opt-in to my eCRM program, which was a conversion rate of 16%, which was 5x my estimated target.
A new site featuring refreshed creative paired with strategic content
Redefined Excedrin.com as the definitive head care resource with a mobile-first, SEO-optimized experience.
WEBSITE STRATEGY  ·  BRAND INTEGRATION  ·  WEBSITE REDESIGN

THE SITUATION
Excedrin, a trusted leader in headache and migraine relief, aimed to redefine its digital presence with a complete redesign of Excedrin.com. The goal was to create a user and mobile-first experience that effectively combined product information, head care education, and user engagement. This project needed to push the boundaries of the existing Haleon Web Framework while adhering to the brand’s refreshed Visual Brand Language (VBL).
THE OPPORTUNITY
With the headache and migraine category being both competitive and cluttered, Excedrin sought to solidify its position as the definitive resource for head care. This presented an opportunity to develop a strong digital platform that would not only educate consumers but also drive conversions. The redesign was focused on three core content pillars: full head health education, practical tips, and new product information. Each pillar was designed to increase organic traffic and guide users smoothly from awareness to purchase.

THE APPROACH
The design focused on delivering a user-centric experience with streamlined navigation, making it easy for users to access the information they needed and find the right products. Interactive tools, like the product selector, simplified decision-making, while strategically placed calls to action guided users from awareness to purchase. SEO-optimized content boosted organic traffic, establishing Excedrin.com as the go-to resource for headache and migraine management. This approach differentiated GSK Consumer Health, inspiring similar strategies across brands. 
The result is a modern, engaging site that educates, engages, and drives conversions, cementing Excedrin’s leadership in the head care category.
Equipping head care features and journeys leading to personalized content for a smooth path to purchase

INTERACTIVE PRODUCT SELECTOR 
This tool guides users to the best Excedrin product for their needs, based on their specific symptoms and preferences, significantly enhancing the decision-making process. 

ENHANCED MOBILE-FIRST DESIGN
The website is fully optimized for mobile devices, ensuring a smooth and responsive experience across all screen sizes, catering to the 81% of users who access the site via mobile. 

DYNAMIC TAGGING FRAMEWORK 
The implementation of a tagging system allows the site to dynamically serve relevant content based on user behavior, such as related articles and product recommendations. 

DYNAMIC PRODUCT COMPARISON TOOL
Allows users to compare different Excedrin products side-by-side, helping them make informed choices with a clear understanding of product benefits and features. 

FULL HEAD HEALTH EDUCATION LIBRARY 
Offers in-depth educational content on headaches, migraines, and related symptoms, providing users with reliable, WebMD-like resources directly within the Excedrin site. 

SMOOTH PATH TO PURCHASE 
Integration with tools like PriceSpider facilitates a smooth transition from information gathering to purchasing, connecting directly to digital retailers.
Increasing HPV Vaccination in Children through "Future is in YOUR Hands"
Refreshed HPV vaccination campaign reaching new audiences and outperforming benchmarks.
OMNICHANNEL DIGITAL + CAMPAIGN STRATEGY  ·  CAMPAIGN SITE  ·  ORGANIC SOCIAL  ·  EARNED MEDIA  ·  PAID MEDIA

THE SITUATION
In partnership with McCann Global Health, St. Jude approached my team in desperate need of a complete refresh of their HPV Vaccination campaign. Not only was the current campaign not gaining the desired momentum and ROI, but it was also dated and geared towards an outdated user group – healthcare providers.
St. Jude had established the need for a refreshed visual look in tandem with a new omnichannel perspective and strategy, in addition to new user audiences, but wasn’t sure where to turn – that’s where my team came in.

THE APPROACH
With My data-backed insights, I immediately determined that the entire campaign was lacking targeted content and journeys for its main audience – parents and caregivers of young adults. My Content, UX, Design, and Media teams worked in tandem to create a wonderfully targeted, beautifully crafted experience that maintained a campaign experience from touchpoint to touchpoint. 
I evaluated key channel pillars to identify not only what should be said, but where it should be said and who it should be targeted to. My team delivered content and strategic areas of focus around education, enablement, and partnership with the St. Jude experience – leading to meaningful, measurable results.

POSITIVE RESULTS
Mid-campaign metrics indicate that the campaign is highly resonant and drastically outperforming benchmarks
I built a strong strategic foundation with targeted content on intentional channels
Paid Social Impressions on Facebook & Instagram
Clicks from paid social to Campaign Website
